doctrine: A product exists when a stranger can name it, see a price, and reach a checkout. Anything less is an artifact.

products.name: Link & QR Safety Report
products.what_it_is: Paste a URL (or a decoded QR payload) -> an OFFLINE heuristic safety report: static redirect-chain extraction, punycode/homoglyph look-alike detection, ~16 known-bad URL patterns (raw-IP host, credential/@ trick, brand look-alikes, abused TLDs, shorteners with unresolved destination, risky download extensions), TLS surface from the URL, and a LOW/ELEVATED/HIGH concern band. Heuristic only, clearly disclaimed — never a guarantee.
products.buyer: Cautious consumers and small-biz owners vetting a link before clicking.

products.name: Compliance Change Digest
products.what_it_is: Given the regulatory/source documents you provide -> a cited, plain-English digest of what changed, who it affects, and an explicit 'what we're uncertain about' section. Citation-per-claim is enforced by a fail-closed linter: an uncited claim, a citation to a non-existent source, or a quote not found verbatim in its source all block the digest from rendering. Reuses the proven hrf-clarity-briefs citation-linter pattern (ported to Node).
products.buyer: Small compliance/ops teams tracking rule changes without a research staff.

products.name: Recurring Charge Finder
products.what_it_is: Upload a bank/card transaction CSV -> every repeating charge the file contains: merchant-normalized grouping, median-interval cadence classification (weekly / biweekly / monthly / quarterly / semiannual / annual / irregular), per-pattern confidence, typical amount, price drift vs the first observed charge, dormancy against the pattern's own cadence, category-tag overlap between distinct merchants, and monthly/annualized cost. Delivered as a 4-sheet XLSX workbook (Summary, Recurring Charges, All Occurrences, Flagged Rows) and a one-page PDF. Deterministic engine; unreadable rows are flagged with line numbers, never dropped. Organizational tooling only.
products.buyer: Solo operators and households who want to see every repeating charge in one place without connecting a bank account.

products.name: Email Header Audit
products.what_it_is: Offline heuristic audit of a pasted email header block: real sender behind the display name (quote-aware, so a decoy address inside a quoted display name does not win), SPF/DKIM/DMARC as REPORTED by a server and explicitly not re-verified, Reply-To/Return-Path/Message-ID/DKIM-domain alignment, punycode + confusable + brand-adjacent sending domains, and the Received delivery path in chronological order. Free ungated preview returns signal COUNTS only; the paid unlock returns every signal with its ordinary benign explanation.
products.buyer: People who get a suspicious-looking email and want to read what the headers actually say, without installing anything or forwarding the message to a third party.

products.name: Client Revenue Concentration Report
products.what_it_is: Upload an invoice CSV -> a deterministic picture of how revenue is distributed across clients: per-client net revenue and share, gross vs credits, average/largest/smallest invoice, months with revenue; top 1/3/5/10 share, HHI on the standard 0-10,000 scale, effective client count (1/sum share^2), and how few clients make up the first 50% and 80%; a monthly revenue series with the largest client per month; per-client dormancy measured against that client’s OWN median invoice gap; payment timing when a paid-date column is present; and multi-spelling client merges that are disclosed rather than hidden. Delivered as a 5-sheet XLSX workbook (Summary, Clients, Monthly Revenue, All Invoices, Flagged Rows) and a one-page PDF. Unreadable rows are flagged with CSV line numbers, never dropped. A free ungated preview returns the top-line figures from the same run.
products.buyer: Freelancers, agencies and solo consultants who want to see how dependent their revenue actually is on a handful of clients, without connecting a bank or accounting system.
products.checkout_blocked_by: FOUNDER GATE: no Stripe price, no env vars, not deployed. Every payment path returns 501 not_configured.
products.entitlement_source: signed Stripe webhook (checkout.session.completed) -> sess:<id> via lib/store.js (Vercel KV when configured, in-memory otherwise)
products.tests: engine.test.js 48/48 pass; buyloop.test.js 32/32 pass (node v22.22.3, no install, no network, no keys)
products.real_vs_stub: REAL: RFC4180 ingest with header aliasing + ISO/US/UK date auto-detection + EU decimals + parenthesised negatives, row flagging with CSV line numbers, client-key normalization (corporate-suffix stripping, &/and folding) with disclosed multi-spelling merges, concentration arithmetic verified against hand-computed cases (single client -> HHI 10000; four equal clients -> HHI 2500), credits netted against the issuing client, net-negative clients excluded from share math but still listed, per-client dormancy against that client’s own median gap, payment timing from a paid-date column (paid-before-invoiced rejected, never negative), monthly series that reconciles to the file total, DEPENDENCY-FREE byte-deterministic XLSX writer and one-page PDF renderer (XLSX verified by walking the central directory and inflating every part; PDF verified by resolving every xref offset to a real object header and checking /Length against true stream bytes), fail-closed advice linter, full checkout->webhook->entitlement->gated-delivery loop, free ungated preview proven to be a genuine subset of the paid run. STUB: no live Stripe account/price/keys and nothing deployed (all payment paths 501 until env is set); Stripe is MOCKED in tests and the store runs in-memory (NOT a live or Stripe-test-mode run); client grouping is string matching only with no company database, so near-identical distinct clients can merge and abbreviations may not; the corporate-suffix list is fixed and Latin-alphabet biased; no enrichment or external lookup of any kind; the in-memory store is not durable across serverless cold starts (KV required before real money).
products.guardrail: organizational tooling only; no financial/tax/legal advice; never tells the user which clients to keep, chase, or drop (enforced by a fail-closed advice linter that runs before any artifact bytes are rendered)
products.linter_improvement: Notes that embed a client name carry a parallel LINT-SAFE twin with the name replaced by <client>; the linter judges the twins. This keeps the engine’s own wording fully guarded while removing the false positive that would withhold a paid report from a buyer whose client is genuinely named e.g. ‘Good Client LLC’. Both halves are covered by tests.
products.known_issue_found: CONFIRMED AGAIN and FIXED HERE ONLY: api/webhook.js in the sibling products (hff-recurring-charges, hff-invoice-aging, hcf-phish-checker, hrf-compliance-digest) assigns module.exports.config BEFORE module.exports = handler, so the second assignment discards config and bodyParser:false never takes effect — which would break EVERY Stripe signature verification in production. This product assigns config AFTER the handler and has a regression test. Siblings remain UNFIXED; writes this run were scoped to this product folder.
products.rung_note: NO FAKE GREEN: code-complete at the doorstep only. products.name: Effective Hourly Rate Report
products.what_it_is: Upload a time-tracking CSV (Toggl/Harvest/Clockify-style export, or any CSV with a date, client and duration) -> what the tracked hours arithmetically earned: per-client hours, share of tracked time, entries, billable vs non-billable split, observed revenue, effective rate per COVERED hour (revenue / hours of the entries that carry billing figures) and blended rate across ALL tracked hours, with the coverage share disclosed so neither number overstates; monthly series (hours, billable share, revenue, active clients) and hours-by-weekday; median and longest sessions; disclosed multi-spelling client merges; negative billed amounts excluded from rate math and disclosed. Durations read as decimal hours, H:MM[:SS], '7h 30m' / '45 min' forms, or a minutes-unit column; when only a rate column exists, figures are computed as rate x hours from the file's own columns and labelled derived. Delivered as a 5-sheet XLSX workbook (Summary, Clients, Monthly, All Entries, Flagged Rows) and a one-page PDF. Unreadable rows are flagged with CSV line numbers, never dropped. A free ungated preview returns the top-line figures from the same run.
products.buyer: Freelancers and consultants who track time and want to see what their hours actually earned, without connecting a bank, an accounting system, or a time-tracking API.

products.entitlement_source: signed Stripe webhook (checkout.session.completed) -> sess:<id> via lib/store.js (Vercel KV when configured, in-memory otherwise)
products.tests: engine.test.js 50/50 pass; buyloop.test.js 32/32 pass (node v22.22.3, no install, no network, no keys)
products.real_vs_stub: REAL: RFC4180 ingest with header aliasing + ISO/US/UK date auto-detection + EU decimals, duration parser (clock/word/decimal/minutes forms; zero and >24h entries flagged, 24h exactly allowed), billable-flag parser with unknown flags counted-but-disclosed rather than guessed, negative-amount exclusion with disclosure, client-key normalization with disclosed merges, rate arithmetic verified against hand-computed cases (450/5=90 covered vs 450/9=50 blended; rate-derived 100/h; amount-beats-rate 150/h), weekday attribution verified against a known calendar date, monthly and per-client series that reconcile exactly to file totals, fail-closed advice linter with <client>-substituted lint-safe twins, DEPENDENCY-FREE byte-deterministic XLSX writer and one-page PDF renderer (XLSX verified by walking the central directory and inflating every part; PDF verified by resolving every xref offset to a real object header and checking /Length against true stream bytes), full checkout->webhook->entitlement->gated-delivery loop, free ungated preview proven to be a genuine subset of the paid run. STUB: no live Stripe account/price/keys and nothing deployed (all payment paths 501 until env is set); Stripe is MOCKED in tests and the store runs in-memory (NOT a live or Stripe-test-mode run); every rate is arithmetic over the uploaded file only — no market-rate data, no benchmarking, no enrichment, no external lookup; client grouping is string matching only with no company database; the in-memory store is not durable across serverless cold starts (KV required before real money).
products.guardrail: organizational tooling only; no financial/tax/legal advice; never tells the user what to charge, never judges a rate (undercharging / too low / below market all banned), never tells the user which clients to keep or drop (enforced by a fail-closed advice linter that runs before any artifact bytes are rendered)
products.webhook_config_note: api/webhook.js assigns module.exports.config AFTER module.exports = handler (the sibling ordering bug is NOT present here) and carries the regression test.
products.rung_note: NO FAKE GREEN: code-complete at the doorstep only. Engine, guardrail linter, free preview and the full checkout->webhook->entitlement->gated-delivery loop are built and tested; Stripe is MOCKED in tests and there are no live keys, no price, and no deployment. $0 earned. ONLY A FOUNDER DEPLOY REMAINS (create $9 one-time price, set env vars incl. Vercel KV before real money, add the webhook endpoint, deploy via scripts/factory_deploy.sh).

non_monetized_factories.note: NON-MONETIZED by design per backend/mission_control/factory_census.py FACTORY_INTENT. These should be EXEMPT from the revenue/earning rollup, not defined as products.
non_monetized_factories.HHF: Hoch Home Personal Factory — serves the Hoch family; success is family utility, not earning.
non_monetized_factories.HPF: Hoch Pods Theater — cinematic swarm-launch visualization; not a product factory.
